text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: COPD, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, blocks airflow through the lungs.
zh: 慢性阻塞性肺病(COPD)会阻塞肺部气流。

text: It makes breathing difficult. The leading cause is cigarette smoking.
zh: 它使呼吸变得困难,主要病因是吸烟。

text: Experts at the National Institutes of Health in the United States
zh: 美国国立卫生研究院的专家们

text: say the damage to the lungs cannot be repaired and there is no cure.
zh: 表示肺部损伤无法修复,也没有治愈方法。

text: Don DeMeo is an assistant professor at Harvard Medical School in Boston,
zh: 唐·德梅奥是波士顿哈佛医学院的助理教授,

text: Massachusetts. Dr. DeMeo says by 2020
zh: 位于马萨诸塞州。德梅奥博士说,到2020年,

text: COPD will likely be the third leading cause of death across the world.
zh: 慢性阻塞性肺病很可能成为全球第三大死因。

text: COPD is a new name for emphysema and chronic bronchitis.
zh: COPD是肺气肿和慢性支气管炎的新名称。

text: These are the two most common forms of the disease.
zh: 这是该疾病最常见的两种形式。

text: Many Many people with COPD have both of them.
zh: 许多COPD患者同时患有这两种病症。

text: And Dr. DeMaio says more women than men now die from the disease.
zh: 德梅奥博士说,如今死于该病的女性多于男性。

text: She is the lead author of a study by a team from Harvard's Brigham and Women's Hospital
zh: 她是哈佛大学布莱根妇女医院团队

text: and the University of Bergen in Norway. way. The new study adds to findings
zh: 与挪威卑尔根大学联合开展的一项研究的第一作者。这项新研究进一步证实了

text: that women may be more at risk than men for the damaging effects of smoking.
zh: 女性可能比男性更容易受到吸烟危害影响的观点。

text: The team examined results from a Norwegian study
zh: 该团队分析了一项挪威研究的结果,

text: of 954 people with COPD.
zh: 该研究涉及954名COPD患者。

text: Inga Cecilia Sorheim co -authored the team's findings.
zh: 英加·塞西莉亚·索海姆是该团队研究成果的共同作者。

text: Dr. Sorheim says they show that women suffered the same severity of COPD
zh: 索海姆博士说,研究显示女性患COPD的严重程度

text: as men, but by comparison, the female smokers were younger and had smoked a lot less.
zh: 与男性相当,但相比之下,女性吸烟者更年轻,吸烟量也少得多。

text: The team also looked at two groups among the people in the study.
zh: 该团队还研究了研究对象中的两个群体。

text: These were people under the age of 60 and those who had smoked for less than 20 years.
zh: 分别是60岁以下的人群和吸烟不足20年的人群。

text: In both cases women had more severe COPD
zh: 在这两种情况下,女性的COPD都更为严重,

text: and a greater loss of lung function than men.
zh: 肺功能的丧失也比男性更明显。

text: Dr. DeMeo says some of the people in the study did not smoke much
zh: 德梅奥博士说,研究中有些人吸烟量并不大,

text: but still developed severe lung disease.
zh: 却依然患上了严重的肺病。

text: She said, Many people underestimate the health risks of their own cigarette smoking.
zh: 她说,很多人低估了自己吸烟带来的健康风险。

text: They think that a few cigarettes every day are harmless.
zh: 他们认为每天抽几支烟无伤大雅。

text: But clearly, there is no such thing as a safe level of cigarette smoke exposure.
zh: 但显然,并不存在所谓“安全”的吸烟暴露水平。

text: And our findings suggest that this is especially true for women.
zh: 我们的研究结果表明,这一点对女性来说尤其如此。

text: Dr. Sorgheim says there are several possible explanations
zh: 索海姆博士说,有几种可能的解释

text: why women may be more at risk from the effects of cigarette smoke than men.
zh: 可以说明为何女性比男性更容易受到香烟烟雾的影响。

text: Women have smaller airways, she she says, so each cigarette may do more harm.
zh: 她说,女性的气道较小,因此每支香烟可能造成更大的伤害。

text: Also, there are differences between males and females
zh: 此外,男性和女性之间

text: in the way the body processes cigarette smoke.
zh: 在身体代谢香烟烟雾的方式上也存在差异。

text: And she says, genes and hormones could also play an important part.
zh: 她还说,基因和激素也可能起到重要作用。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 以上就是本期美国之音特别英语健康报道。
